next_action: Prove the ballot lemma for smooth GP excursions: P(positive excursion of length g | slope y) ~ c*y as y->0. This gives alpha<=1, hence chi(3) bound, hence CV>=0.422>0.361, closing Step 6 analytically. Search Azais-Wschebor Ch.3, Leadbetter-Lindgren-Rootzen, or derive from Slepian linearization.
context_notes: Session 13 was a marathon (13 commits). Started with hand-wavy Step 3, ended with 10-step proof where 9 steps are theorems and 1 step (the ballot lemma) is the remaining analytical gap. The chi(3) framework explains WHY the CV bound holds: the excursion probability reweighting turns the Rayleigh into a chi(3) distribution near y=0. Key dead ends: quadratic test function (wrong sign), Taylor expansion (diverges), strict induction (oscillations), Cauchy-Schwarz on correlation (fails by 2x). Venv still broken; use /c/Python313/python.exe. MiKTeX now installed.
